what_it_does: Documents the deployed Jina Embeddings v4 model on the local substrate: its architecture, runtime environment, serving configuration, capabilities, quirks, tested performance, open questions, and links to related models and runtimes.
observations: Multimodal embedding model for text, images, and visual documents with 2048-d BF16 weights and a 32,768-token native context.
Served via a dedicated Python venv (jina-v4-env) because vLLM 0.21 cannot load the JinaEmbeddingsV4Model dynamic HF module + peft LoRAs.
Requires transformers<5.0 because the bundled qwen2_5_vl.py accesses a ROPE init key removed in transformers 5.x.
Uses task-specific LoRA adapters for retrieval, text-matching, and code, plus asymmetric query/passage prompt modes.
The FastAPI server serializes GPU access with a global asyncio lock, deliberately capping concurrency at 1.
Co-resident with qwen3_5-4b-awq and bge-m3 at roughly 15.5 GiB total VRAM.
Multi-vector output mode is supported by the model but not exposed by the current server.
Visual-document use cases (PDFs with layout, charts, tables) are claimed but not exercised end-to-end.
standouts: It is the only substrate model explicitly built for text+image+visual-document embeddings.
It must live in a separate, transformers-pinned venv because vLLM cannot load its architecture.
The server intentionally limits GPU access to a single stream via an asyncio lock.
conventions: Follows the model-card frontmatter pattern with type, id, status, runtime, port, date_added, and tags.
Uses wiki-style internal links and a backlink index hub.
Breaks the default vLLM-serving convention by using a custom FastAPI server in a separate venv.
concerns: Concurrency is capped at 1 by a global lock, with no measured batch or multi-stream throughput.
Multi-vector output and visual-document capabilities are advertised but not exposed or tested.
Wrong task or prompt-mode selection silently degrades embedding quality.
No precise latency numbers for subsequent calls or batched requests.
notes: A deployed multimodal embedding model card that explains why it runs outside the main vLLM environment and how it is served.
questions: What exact transformers version is pinned in jina-v4-env?
How does the server accept and preprocess image and PDF inputs?
How are the three LoRA adapters selected and loaded at runtime?
What is the latency and throughput when batching larger request lists?
What would the multi-vector response shape look like if exposed?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose (document a deployed multimodal embedding model) matches the content; it also acts as an operational runbook for its non-standard serving setup.
intention_signals: To record not just what the model is, but how to keep it running on this specific substrate despite upstream loader incompatibilities, and to flag which capabilities are still untapped.
novelty: It is the only substrate model card explicitly covering visual-document embedding and the only one documenting a model that must be served in a separate, transformers-pinned venv because vLLM cannot load it.
contribution: A self-contained operational record of a non-vLLM multimodal embedding service, including its runtime constraints, LoRA task/prompt semantics, co-residency footprint, and unexercised capabilities, so a fused view can account for it without re-deriving the serving workaround.
summary_for_embedding: Model card for the deployed jinaai/jina-embeddings-v4 multimodal embedding model, describing its 2048-d BF16 architecture, jina-v4-env serving setup, LoRA task adapters, query/passage prompt modes, single-stream GPU lock, and open visual-document/multi-vector items.
intention_for_embedding: To document how this specific multimodal embedding model is integrated, constrained, and operated on the local substrate.
